name: Chilled Puree of Carrot and Melon
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a stock pot over low heat. Add the onions and cook slowly, stirring for about 7 minutes without letting them color.
2. Add the carrots and continue cooking, stirring until most of their water has been released and they fall apart, approximately 35 minutes.
3. Add the chicken stock, cover, bring to a boil, reduce the heat, and simmer for about 15 minutes.
4. Add the cantaloupe, salt, coriander, and cumin; cook for 2 minutes.
5. Strain the mixture and set aside.
6. Transfer the carrots, onions, and cantaloupe to a blender or food processor and puree until very smooth.
7. Return the puree to the pot and add the reserved liquid. Chill in the refrigerator for 2-to-3 hours.
8. Combine the milk and yogurt in a small bowl until smooth. Reserve until serving.
9. Just before serving, pour the soup into chilled soup bowls. Decorate the surface by spooning three simple lines of the yogurt mixture across each bowl of soup. Using the handle end of the spoon, lightly drag across the lines at a 90-degree angle to create a "feathered" decoration.
